headline: Man's 'I Don't Need A Manual' While Assembling IKEA Results In 'Postmodern Bookshelf' Leaning 47 Degrees
summary: He calls it 'intentional asymmetry.' His girlfriend calls it 'a hazard.' She's sleeping on the couch.
fullBody: Jake McDIY refused the IKEA instructions. 'I have intuition,' Jake said. 6 hours later, he had extra screws, a missing shelf, and a bookshelf that leans aggressively. 'It's conceptual,' Jake claimed. His girlfriend has hired a professional. The professional laughed. The shelf is now 'art.' Jake is no longer allowed to assemble furniture. He's now 'supervising' — which means 'offering unhelpful suggestions from the couch.'
